# Template Customization Guide

## Purpose
Provide guidance for tailoring templates to organizational needs while maintaining consistency, governance, and quality standards.

## Audience
PMO, Project Managers, Process Owners, Template Librarians, Stakeholders.

## 1. Customization Philosophy
- **Start with proven templates:** use the shared library as the baseline.
- **Customize incrementally:** make small changes and validate with users.
- **Maintain consistency:** align terminology, fields, and reporting formats.
- **Focus on value:** only add sections that drive decisions or compliance.
- **Document changes:** track what changed and why.

## 2. Template Categories & Priorities
### Essential Templates (Customize First)
- Project charter.
- Stakeholder register.
- Communications plan.
- Status report.

### Industry-Specific Templates (Customize Next)
- Compliance or regulatory templates.
- Industry risk registers.
- Specialized quality plans.

### Advanced Templates (Customize Last)
- Infrastructure or architecture templates.
- DevOps and release governance templates.
- Hybrid or complex governance packs.

## 3. Customization Process
### Phase 1: Assessment & Planning
- [ ] Inventory templates in use.
- [ ] Gather feedback from users and reviewers.
- [ ] Document organization-specific requirements.
- [ ] Prioritize templates by usage and impact.

### Phase 2: Core Template Customization
- [ ] Add company branding and terminology.
- [ ] Insert approval workflows and governance gates.
- [ ] Align with reporting cadence and tool integrations.
- [ ] Pilot with select projects and gather feedback.

### Phase 3: Extended Customization
- [ ] Customize industry-specific sections.
- [ ] Integrate compliance requirements.
- [ ] Build cross-template references.
- [ ] Roll out training and enablement.

### Phase 4: Continuous Improvement
- [ ] Review usage metrics and feedback quarterly.
- [ ] Apply iterative updates and versioning.
- [ ] Retire or consolidate low-value templates.

## 4. Formatting & Branding Checklist
- [ ] Add logo, header/footer, and document metadata.
- [ ] Standardize fonts, colors, and table layouts.
- [ ] Align naming conventions with governance.
- [ ] Include document control and approval tables.

## 5. Content Customization Checklist
- [ ] Align terminology to organizational roles and systems.
- [ ] Map responsibilities to actual governance structure.
- [ ] Add organization-specific risk categories and KPIs.
- [ ] Ensure compliance language is current.

## 6. Integration Guidance
- **Tool alignment:** ensure fields map to scheduling, finance, and reporting systems.
- **Workflow automation:** connect approval steps to workflow tools where possible.
- **Data standards:** define required fields and validation rules.
- **Retention & archive:** align with records management policies.

## 7. Quality Assurance
### Template Validation
- [ ] Completeness review (required sections present).
- [ ] Consistency check across templates.
- [ ] Usability testing with pilot teams.
- [ ] Compliance review for regulatory templates.

### Ongoing Quality Management
- [ ] Establish review cadence and owners.
- [ ] Track change requests and decisions.
- [ ] Maintain version history and release notes.

## 8. Governance & Change Control
- **Template owner:** accountable for updates and approvals.
- **Subject matter experts:** review content accuracy.
- **Quality reviewer:** ensure consistency and usability.
- **Change control:** define request intake, review, approval, and rollout steps.

## 9. Success Metrics
### Quantitative Metrics
- Template adoption rate.
- Time saved per project.
- Reduction in rework or audit findings.

### Qualitative Metrics
- User satisfaction and feedback.
- Improved decision quality.
- Increased governance compliance.

## 10. Related Templates
- Template catalog (`docs/product/templates-catalog.md`).
- Project management plan (`docs/templates/governance/project-management-plan-cross.md`).
- Communications plan (`docs/templates/communications/communications-plan-template-cross.md`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Customization approach is documented and approved.
- Templates remain consistent, governed, and easy to use.
- Metrics exist to validate template effectiveness.
